hud-fidelity.reasoning: Both terminal tests now pass cleanly. Sparkline test: U+2581–U+2588 alphabet, 8 cells, fixed, in-line; the ▁-floor makes zero a visible baseline rather than absence-of-band. Spellcheck test: column-1 SEV_ERROR displacement marks failed work on the line of work itself and accumulates as a vertical red stripe under load — the canonical Tufte gutter-mass exception count, here delivered. Per-row primitives stay at ≤3 in the typical eyebrow mode (gutter `●` + verb glyph + description); chip mode (interleaving) and error-heavy freshest row brush the ceiling at 4 visual elements but the candidate explicitly trades off and isolates this state. The seed's four data-ink offences (freshest double-encoding, `7/min` redundancy, open verb dictionary, primitive-count creep) are all addressed.
hud-fidelity.improvement: Drop the `●` gutter from the freshest row when SEV_ERROR is already displaying in column 1 — under error-heavy load the freshest row currently shows both, which is the same double-encoding offence the iteration solved everywhere else.

four-questions.reasoning: All four answerable by glance. Liveness from sparkline shape with ▁-floor distinguishing 'rendering at zero' from 'panel collapsed'. Pattern from sparkline burst/decay/flat shapes plus the verb-glyph stripe acting as a second pattern instrument. Exception cleanly answered by column-1 SEV_ERROR displacement that bypasses tier-slide on unfocus and forms vertical mass under cascading failures. Latest answered by gutter `●` (over-encoded only on the error-heavy freshest row). One soft demerit: Pattern still partially conflates 'stuck' (recent-but-unchanging within the 64s window) with 'idle' (post-burst decay) — both render `▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁` after 64s of inactivity, with no second instrument distinguishing them. Iteration-2 territory.
four-questions.improvement: Add a single age-of-freshest-entry annotation (e.g. relative-time tick on the sparkline's right edge or the eyebrow line) to disambiguate stuck from idle without adding a primitive — but do this only if it doesn't push primitive count over 3.

terminal-feasibility.reasoning: Implementable in OpenTUI today. All six new MD-PUA codepoints verified (nf-md-record / eye / format-list-bulleted / magnify / pencil / play) — single-cell, EAW Neutral, in budget. SEV_ERROR (U+F0028) reused for failed-row column-1 displacement at zero budget cost. Sparkline contract pinned across all six dimensions; verb-glyph dictionary closed at 5 with a 4-rule fallback; hybrid eyebrow/chip source rule resolves multi-source thrash; unfocus tier-slide committed. Three concrete tomorrow-build stalls remain that block straight-through implementation: (1) system-tagged source `[bell]` precedence rule against the run-length-keyed chip/eyebrow logic is undefined; (2) the existing `splitOutcome()` regex still parses trailing `(passed)`/`(failed)` markers from the message — no bridge between that legacy pipeline and the new column-1 displacement; (3) off-by-2 column accounting in the 30-col mocks (the candidate's annotations describe a 30-col outer width but the mock content is laid out in a 28-cell inner area after pad-1+pad-1). One iteration-2 design call: same-agent multi-thread case (`pi 15c8` + `pi 9a01` would render `pi│ pi│` in chip mode because threadid suffix is dropped from the chip — agent-code is the only mnemonic).
terminal-feasibility.improvement: Add three single-section edits: (a) system-tag chip rule before the run-length rule (`source.startsWith('[') → render the tag inline as a 1-cell tone-coloured glyph in column 1, no chip`); (b) explicit splitOutcome bridge note in §Verb-glyph column rule (a) (when tone===error AND splitOutcome detects `(failed)`, strip the suffix from the displayed message and render SEV_ERROR in column 1, so the legacy producer doesn't dual-mark); (c) fix the column arithmetic in §States so 30-col outer = 28-cell inner content.

asi: Iteration-1 closed seven of the seed's eight foundational gaps and lifted the design to 8.3 composite. The remaining gap is buildability-at-edges: three single-section edits would convert the spec from 'buildable with three known stalls' to 'buildable straight through.' Specifically (a) precedence rule for system-tagged sources `[bell]`/`[event]` against the run-length-keyed chip/eyebrow logic — render the tag as a 1-cell tone-coloured glyph in column 1, ahead of the chip/eyebrow branching; (b) splitOutcome bridge — when tone===error AND the legacy `splitOutcome()` regex detects a trailing `(failed)`/`(passed)` marker, strip the suffix from the displayed description and rely on the column-1 SEV_ERROR displacement (or no marker for `(passed)`) so the legacy producer pipeline never dual-marks an outcome row; (c) fix the off-by-2 column accounting in §States so the 30-col mocks reflect the actual 28-cell inner content area after pad-1+pad-1. All three are zero-vocab, zero-producer, single-section edits inside existing subsections. Optional secondary tightening for hud-fidelity 9→10: drop the `●` gutter on the freshest row when column 1 is displaying SEV_ERROR (avoids the only remaining double-encoding under error-heavy load). Defer for iteration-3 (or user design call): same-agent multi-thread chip collision (`pi│ pi│`) — the fix forks across multiple design choices (3-char tied tier vs 2-char threadid-suffix `p8│` vs fall-through to eyebrow), each with trade-offs.
